description: A round three-dimensional shape, like a ball.
example: The Earth is shaped like a sphere.

description: Moved air from the mouth, or was moved by the wind; past tense of blow.
example: The wind blew the leaves across the path.

description: A cart or small vehicle with wheels used for carrying things.
example: We put the food in the trolley at the shop.

description: A long journey, especially by sea or through space.
example: The sailors went on a long voyage across the ocean.

description: The smallest amount or the lowest degree.
example: I have the least amount of paint left.

description: For what reason.
example: Why is the sky blue?

description: A device that records or makes your voice louder.
example: She sang into the microphone.

description: A soft piece of paper used for wiping your nose; also a type of body material.
example: I used a tissue to wipe my nose.

description: Food from an animal.
example: The dog ate a piece of meat.

description: The part of the face with the mouth and teeth.
example: The dentist looked at my jaw.

description: A flat object with raised edges used for carrying things.
example: She carried the cups on a tray.

description: Sliding boxes in furniture used for storing things.
example: I put my socks in the drawers.

description: A colour like the sky on a clear day.
example: The boat is blue.

description: Long hairs on an animal's face, especially near the nose or mouth.
example: The cat has long whiskers.

description: A dull, heavy thumping sound.
example: The bag landed with a whump.

description: A coloured wax stick used for drawing.
example: I drew a sun with a yellow crayon.

description: A metal fastener that turns into wood or another material; also to twist something into place.
example: Dad used a screw to fix the shelf.

description: An animal that often climbs trees and has a long tail.
example: The monkey climbed the tree.

description: A three-dimensional shape with six equal square faces.
example: The dice is shaped like a cube.

description: The place where someone lives.
example: We went home after school.

description: A small imaginary creature often shown as an old man with a pointed hat.
example: There is a garden gnome by the flowers.

description: In or into the inner part of something.
example: The toy is inside the box.

description: The number 5.
example: I have five crayons.

description: A piece of clothing worn on the upper body.
example: He wore a red shirt.

description: Working by itself with little or no human help.
example: The automatic door opened by itself.

description: To get away from a place or danger.
example: The rabbit tried to escape from the garden.

description: A device used to slow or stop a vehicle.
example: Mum pressed the brake to stop the car.

description: Coming before all others.
example: She was first in the line.

description: A reddish-brown coating that forms on iron or steel.
example: The old bike had rust on it.

description: The season after summer and before winter.
example: Leaves fall from the trees in autumn.

description: Activities played for fun, often with rules.
example: We played games after dinner.

description: An animal with a horse as one parent and a donkey as the other.
example: The mule carried the bags up the hill.

description: Finished, or with no parts missing.
example: The puzzle is complete.

description: A round root vegetable; also a person from Sweden.
example: We ate swede with our dinner.

description: A sea animal with a rough shell.
example: The oyster was inside its shell.

description: Made an effort to do something; past tense of try.
example: I tried to tie my shoes.

description: An old-fashioned word said to show disapproval.
example: Fie, that was not kind!

description: On every side of something, or moving in a circle.
example: We walked around the pond.

description: Great sadness or trouble.
example: The story was full of woe.

description: Fastened with a knot or string; past tense of tie.
example: She tied the ribbon in a bow.

description: People admired for being brave or doing good things.
example: The firefighters were heroes.

description: The opening in the face used for eating, speaking, and breathing.
example: Open your mouth wide.

description: An enemy or opponent.
example: The knight faced his foe.
